parsed_review.summary:
Independent read-only review of CP00-200 (RP06 DMS Core, "Permission And Audit Binding", risk B, 40 units, RP06.P01.M04.S07-RP06.P01.M06.S04). The pack is descriptor-only and internally consistent; I find no P0/P1 issues and recommend PASS, with one trivial documentation nit (P3).

Verification performed via Read/Grep/Glob only (no Bash, no writes, no connectors):
- Pack identity: registry.js DMS_CORE_CP200_PACK_BINDING matches baseline exactly (pack_id CP00-200, risk B, 40 units, range, upstream CP00-199 -> next CP00-201, next_subphase RP06.P01.M06.S05, gates H06/C06).
- Deliverable distribution hand-counted from the 40 baseline included_units: implementation 22, ui 6, fixture 2, test 6, hermes_evidence 2, claude_review 2 (=40); micro_title distribution Secondary Workflow Slice 14 / Permission And Audit Binding 22 / Synthetic Fixture Set 4. Both match the stated distributions.
- No-write / no-real-data boundary: DMS_CORE_CP200_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (registry.js:98-107) inherits and extends all-false runtime/exposure/real-data flags (writes_product_state, evaluates_runtime_permission, writes_audit_event, dispatches_permission_runtime, dispatches_audit_runtime, exposes_permission_envelope_payload, exposes_audit_trace_payload, uses_real_*_data, implements_citation_ledger_runtime, implements_loop_engine) with synthetic_only/descriptor-only true. README.md:17 and service.js closeout_handoff reaffirm persistence, object storage, OCR, search, email, Citation Ledger, and Loop runtime remain out of scope. Grep of packages/dms/src confirms no fetch/fs/network/SQL/secret usage.
- Required public exports: all 10 CP00-200 functions are defined across registry/service/validators and reachable through index.js wildcard re-exports.
- Tests: model.test.js carries four dedicated CP00-200 tests (plan-binding coverage of 40 units, permission/audit binding descriptor-only, fixture integrity incl. invalid-reference + ownership-drift + no-leak, and contract evidence packets/handoff to CP00-201).
- Authority boundary upheld: Claude packet (service.js:560-567) is read_only:true with source_inspection_basis read_tools_used and forbidden_review_evidence wired as invalid-review blockers; validators enforce promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false. Claude is the read-only independent reviewer, not final approver.
- Hermes usage is control-plane only: hermes_packet receipt_shape "descriptor_only_permission_audit_fixture_no_write" — deterministic review packet/receipt handling, not embedded product runtime.
- CP178+ evidence-hardening: readiness-report auth_preflight passed (exit 0, 1445-byte valid-JSON stdout) — no auth failure, no 0-byte stdout, no malformed/fenced/tool-shaped output. summary_only_review_allowed is false (Risk B + review-evidence deliverables present); this review used read tools against source as required.

On the production_ready claim: the manifest is status in_progress / production_ready false, and command-evidence, adjudication, construction-inspection, and claude-review-result are pending. These are the expected pre-review states — this read-only review (read_only_claude_raw_capture) precedes adjudication, inspection, and final_closeout_validation in the hardened sequence. Critically, the pack makes no premature production_ready or enterprise-trust assertion, so there is no unsupported claim to block on; the production_ready flip and enterprise-trust sign-off correctly remain gated on the downstream human-approved steps.

findings.title: documentation_entry points at a README anchor that does not resolve
findings.details: createDmsCoreCp200PermissionAuditDescriptor sets documentation_entry to "packages/dms/README.md#cp00-200". The CP00-200 content does exist in packages/dms/README.md (an unheaded paragraph at line 17), but the file's only heading is "# DMS Package" (line 1). There is no "## CP00-200" (or equivalent) heading, so the "#cp00-200" fragment does not correspond to any generated Markdown anchor. This is a cosmetic descriptor-string inaccuracy only: the documentation is present, no test or validator asserts anchor resolution, and it has no effect on the descriptor-only/no-write behavior, the permission/audit binding evidence, or any boundary attestation.
findings.recommendation: Add a "## CP00-200" heading above the existing CP00-200 paragraph in packages/dms/README.md (so the "#cp00-200" anchor resolves), or change documentation_entry to a plain file reference without a fragment (e.g. "packages/dms/README.md"). Non-blocking; can be folded into CP00-201 documentation work.
findings.post_review_disposition: Accepted as a non-blocking P3 documentation nit. Does not block pack or goal closeout; logged for cleanup in/after CP00-201. No re-review required.
